name: Hans Christian Andersen
description: The world's most beloved fairy tale writer whose stories like 'The Little Mermaid' and 'The Ugly Duckling' transcend culture and age
notable_works: The Little Mermaid; The Ugly Duckling; Thumbelina; The Snow Queen; The Little Match Girl

name: Niels Bohr
description: Nobel Prize-winning physicist who made foundational contributions to atomic structure and quantum theory
notable_works: Model of the atom (1913); Correspondence principle; Copenhagen interpretation of quantum mechanics

name: Søren Kierkegaard
description: Pioneer of existentialist philosophy who profoundly influenced theology, psychology and literature
notable_works: Either/Or; Fear and Trembling; The Sickness Unto Death; Concluding Unscientific Postscript

name: Lars von Trier
description: Provocateur auteur filmmaker and co-founder of the Dogme 95 movement known for emotionally intense cinema
notable_works: Breaking the Waves; Dancer in the Dark; Melancholia; Nymphomaniac; The House That Jack Built

name: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: Creator of the C++ programming language, one of the world's most widely used programming languages
notable_works: C++ programming language; The C++ Programming Language (book)

name: Tycho Brahe
description: Renaissance astronomer whose precise naked-eye observations revolutionized astronomy before telescopes
notable_works: Tychonic system; Supernova observations; Precise star catalog

name: Karen Blixen
description: Author who wrote under pen name Isak Dinesen; famed for her memoir of life on a Kenyan coffee plantation
notable_works: Out of Africa; Seven Gothic Tales; Babette's Feast; Winter's Tales

name: Viggo Mortensen
description: Academy Award-nominated actor known globally for his role as Aragorn in Lord of the Rings
notable_works: Lord of the Rings trilogy; A History of Violence; Eastern Promises; Green Book; Falling
